title: AC/DC Concept Model
description: Analysis Concept / Data Concept framework for clinical trials data

nodes.name: Datum
nodes.desc: The unifying supertype for all data in the AC/DC pipeline. A Datum is any structured piece of information that carries provenance, context, and meaning within a clinical study.

nodes.name: Study
nodes.desc: Clinical study/trial providing protocol context.

nodes.name: Subject
nodes.desc: Individual enrolled in a study.

nodes.name: TrialDesign
nodes.desc: Protocol design elements: arms, elements, visits, sets, I/E criteria.

nodes.name: Device
nodes.desc: Medical device under study.

nodes.name: NonHostOrganism
nodes.desc: Non-host organism (vector, pathogen).

nodes.name: SubjectPool
nodes.desc: Pool of subjects for non-assignable results.

nodes.name: Observation
nodes.desc: A single collected fact about a Subject within a study domain. The atomic unit of SDTM.

nodes.name: EventObservation
nodes.desc: Observation about a clinical event (AE, MH, CE, HO, DS). Topic: --TERM.

nodes.name: InterventionObservation
nodes.desc: Observation about a treatment/medication/procedure (CM, EX, SU, EC, PR, AG). Topic: --TRT.

nodes.name: FindingObservation
nodes.desc: Observation from a measurement/test/exam (LB, VS, EG, PE, QS). Topic: --TESTCD/--TEST.

nodes.name: FindingAboutObservation
nodes.desc: A Finding about an Event or Intervention via --OBJ (FA, SR domains).

nodes.name: Topic
nodes.desc: Core subject matter: --TERM (Events), --TRT (Interventions), --TESTCD/--TEST (Findings).

nodes.name: DictionaryCoding
nodes.desc: Hierarchical dictionary mapping: MedDRA (LLT->PT->HLT->HLGT->SOC), WHODrug.

nodes.name: Result
nodes.desc: Finding value: original and standard results with ranges and flags.

nodes.name: SeverityAssessment
nodes.desc: Severity, causality, outcome, action taken, toxicity grade for Events.

nodes.name: SeriousnessCriteria
nodes.desc: SAE criteria flags for serious adverse events.

nodes.name: DoseInformation
nodes.desc: Dosing for Interventions: amount, unit, form, frequency, route.

nodes.name: ObservationTiming
nodes.desc: Full temporal context: dates, study days, visits, epochs, time points.

nodes.name: AnatomicalContext
nodes.desc: Location, laterality, directionality, portion/totality.

nodes.name: Specimen
nodes.desc: Specimen material, condition, usability.

nodes.name: CollectionMethod
nodes.desc: Method/instrument for data collection.

nodes.name: CompletionStatus
nodes.desc: Collection status and completion indicators.

nodes.name: Evaluator
nodes.desc: Subjective assessment evaluator.

nodes.name: SupplementalQualifier
nodes.desc: Non-standard qualifier from SUPPQUAL dataset.

nodes.name: RelatedRecord
nodes.desc: Cross-domain record linkage via RELREC.

nodes.name: Derivation
nodes.desc: A derived datum computed from Observations. IS_A Datum. The unit of ADaM (BDS, OCCDS).

nodes.name: DerivedMeasure
nodes.desc: The derived analytical measurement: parameter identification and computed values (baseline, change, shift, criteria).

nodes.name: DerivationTiming
nodes.desc: Derived temporal context: imputed dates, relative days, visits, periods, phases, windows.

nodes.name: DerivationFlag
nodes.desc: Derived indicators: baseline, treatment-emergent, on-treatment, analysis selection flags.

nodes.name: OccurrenceClassification
nodes.desc: OCCDS: first-occurrence logic, max-severity, imputed severity/causality/toxicity, SMQ/CQ.

nodes.name: TreatmentContext
nodes.desc: Record-level treatment: planned, actual, pooled, dose information.

nodes.name: RangeAssessment
nodes.desc: Toxicity grading, range indicators, normal ranges.

nodes.name: PopulationIndicator
nodes.desc: Population flags at subject, record, and parameter levels.

nodes.name: TimeToEvent
nodes.desc: Time-to-event specific variables: origin, censoring, event descriptions.

nodes.name: Inference
nodes.desc: An analytical conclusion produced by applying a statistical method to Derivations. IS_A Datum.

nodes.name: SummaryStatistic
nodes.desc: Computed data summaries: rounding, quantiles, skewness, kurtosis.

nodes.name: GeneralLinearModel
nodes.desc: GLM family: t-tests, ANOVA, ANCOVA, MANOVA, linear regression.

nodes.name: GeneralizedLinearModel
nodes.desc: GLZMs: logistic, Poisson, negative binomial regression.

nodes.name: NonParametricTest
nodes.desc: Rank-based tests: Wilcoxon, Mann-Whitney, Kruskal-Wallis, Friedman.

nodes.name: CategoricalTest
nodes.desc: Categorical data tests: Chi-square, Fisher's exact, McNemar's, CMH.

nodes.name: RepeatedMeasures
nodes.desc: Correlated data: MMRM (Linear Mixed Model), GEE.

nodes.name: SurvivalModel
nodes.desc: Time-to-event: Kaplan-Meier, log-rank, Cox PH.

nodes.name: CorrelationAnalysis
nodes.desc: Association measures: Pearson, Spearman, Kendall correlation.

nodes.name: SampleSizeCalculation
nodes.desc: Sample size/power: Superiority, Equivalence, Non-inferiority.

nodes.name: CausalInference
nodes.desc: Causal/ML methods: Propensity Score Matching, PCA, Clustering.

nodes.name: CrossLanguageDifference
nodes.desc: 52 documented behavioral differences between R, SAS, Python for the same method.

nodes.name: OntologyMapping
nodes.desc: STATO ontology alignment for analysis methods.

nodes.name: OneSampleTTest
nodes.desc: One-sample t-test for comparing sample mean to known value.

nodes.name: PairedTTest
nodes.desc: Paired t-test for comparing means of matched samples.

nodes.name: TwoSampleTTest
nodes.desc: Two-sample t-test for comparing means of independent groups.

nodes.name: ANOVA
nodes.desc: Analysis of Variance for comparing means across multiple groups.

nodes.name: ANCOVA
nodes.desc: Analysis of Covariance combining ANOVA with regression covariates.

nodes.name: MANOVA
nodes.desc: Multivariate Analysis of Variance for multiple dependent variables.

nodes.name: LinearRegression
nodes.desc: Linear regression for modeling continuous outcomes.

nodes.name: LogisticRegression
nodes.desc: Logistic regression for binary outcomes.

nodes.name: PoissonRegression
nodes.desc: Poisson regression for count data.

nodes.name: NegativeBinomialRegression
nodes.desc: Negative binomial regression for overdispersed count data.

nodes.name: WilcoxonSignedRank
nodes.desc: Wilcoxon signed-rank test for paired samples.

nodes.name: MannWhitney
nodes.desc: Mann-Whitney U test (Wilcoxon rank-sum) for independent samples.

nodes.name: KruskalWallis
nodes.desc: Kruskal-Wallis test for comparing multiple independent groups.

nodes.name: Friedman
nodes.desc: Friedman test for comparing multiple related samples.

nodes.name: ChiSquare
nodes.desc: Chi-square test for independence in contingency tables.

nodes.name: FisherExact
nodes.desc: Fisher's exact test for small sample contingency tables.

nodes.name: McNemar
nodes.desc: McNemar's test for paired nominal data.

nodes.name: CochranMantelHaenszel
nodes.desc: Cochran-Mantel-Haenszel test for stratified categorical data.

nodes.name: MMRM
nodes.desc: Mixed Model for Repeated Measures.

nodes.name: GEE
nodes.desc: Generalized Estimating Equations for correlated data.

nodes.name: KaplanMeier
nodes.desc: Kaplan-Meier survival curve estimation.

nodes.name: LogRank
nodes.desc: Log-rank test for comparing survival curves.

nodes.name: CoxProportionalHazards
nodes.desc: Cox proportional hazards regression for survival analysis.

nodes.name: PearsonCorrelation
nodes.desc: Pearson product-moment correlation coefficient.

nodes.name: SpearmanCorrelation
nodes.desc: Spearman rank correlation coefficient.

nodes.name: KendallCorrelation
nodes.desc: Kendall's tau correlation coefficient.

nodes.name: SampleSizeSuperiority
nodes.desc: Sample size calculation for superiority trials.

nodes.name: SampleSizeEquivalence
nodes.desc: Sample size calculation for equivalence trials.

nodes.name: SampleSizeNonInferiority
nodes.desc: Sample size calculation for non-inferiority trials.

nodes.name: PropensityScoreMatching
nodes.desc: Propensity score matching for causal inference.

nodes.name: Rounding
nodes.desc: Rounding methods with cross-language differences. R uses banker's rounding by default.

nodes.name: QuantilePercentile
nodes.desc: Quantile/percentile calculation with 9 types in R, 5 in SAS.

nodes.name: Skewness
nodes.desc: Skewness calculation.

nodes.name: Kurtosis
nodes.desc: Kurtosis calculation.

nodes.name: P-Value
nodes.desc: Probability value for hypothesis testing. Common output across most statistical tests.

nodes.name: ConfidenceInterval
nodes.desc: Confidence interval for parameter estimates.

nodes.name: EffectEstimate
nodes.desc: Point estimate of treatment effect or group difference.

nodes.name: T-Statistic
nodes.desc: T-statistic from t-tests and related methods.

nodes.name: F-Statistic
nodes.desc: F-statistic from ANOVA and related methods.

nodes.name: ChiSquareStatistic
nodes.desc: Chi-square statistic from categorical tests.

nodes.name: HazardRatio
nodes.desc: Hazard ratio from survival analysis.

nodes.name: OddsRatio
nodes.desc: Odds ratio from logistic regression and categorical analyses.

nodes.name: SurvivalCurve
nodes.desc: Survival probability curve from Kaplan-Meier.

nodes.name: CorrelationCoefficient
nodes.desc: Correlation coefficient from association analyses.

nodes.name: LSMean
nodes.desc: Least-squares mean (adjusted mean) from ANCOVA/MMRM.

nodes.name: SampleSizeResult
nodes.desc: Sample size calculation result.

nodes.name: QuantitativeResult
nodes.desc: A numeric measurement value - the quantitative outcome of a measurement or derivation.

nodes.name: QualitativeResult
nodes.desc: A categorical or text value - non-numeric representation of a measurement or derivation.

nodes.name: MeasurementUnit
nodes.desc: Unit of measurement for a quantitative result (e.g., mg/dL, mmHg, kg).

nodes.name: ReferenceRange
nodes.desc: Expected or normal value bounds for a measurement, defining clinically acceptable limits.

nodes.name: RangeClassification
nodes.desc: Classification of a result relative to reference range (LOW, NORMAL, HIGH).

nodes.name: ToxicityGrade
nodes.desc: Severity grading of adverse findings per CTCAE or similar toxicity scale (Grade 1-5).

nodes.name: SeverityLevel
nodes.desc: Intensity evaluation of an event or finding (MILD, MODERATE, SEVERE).

nodes.name: CausalityAssessment
nodes.desc: Assessment of relationship between an event and study treatment.

nodes.name: TemporalAnchor
nodes.desc: A specific point in time when an observation or derivation occurred.

nodes.name: RelativeTime
nodes.desc: Time expressed as days (or other units) from a reference anchor date.

nodes.name: VisitContext
nodes.desc: The scheduled or actual study visit associated with an observation.

nodes.name: StudyPhase
nodes.desc: Phase or epoch of the study (SCREENING, TREATMENT, FOLLOW-UP).

nodes.name: BaselineIndicator
nodes.desc: Flag indicating whether a record represents the baseline measurement.

nodes.name: IndicatorFlag
nodes.desc: A categorical flag indicating a status or classification for a record. Parent concept for all flag types.

nodes.name: DerivationIndicator
nodes.desc: Flag indicating whether a value was derived rather than directly collected.

nodes.name: BaselineValue
nodes.desc: The reference point value for comparison - the subject's value at baseline for a parameter.

nodes.name: ChangeFromBaseline
nodes.desc: Absolute change computed as analysis value minus baseline (AVAL - BASE).

nodes.name: PercentChange
nodes.desc: Relative change from baseline expressed as percentage ((AVAL-BASE)/BASE)*100.

nodes.name: RatioToBaseline
nodes.desc: Proportional comparison to baseline (AVAL / BASE).

nodes.name: ShiftCategory
nodes.desc: Categorical transition from baseline to post-baseline (e.g., 'NORMAL to HIGH').

nodes.name: DerivationMethod
nodes.desc: How the analysis value was imputed or derived (LOCF, WOCF, AVERAGE, etc.).

nodes.name: AnalysisCriterion
nodes.desc: Pre-specified condition to evaluate (e.g., 'SYSBP > 140').

nodes.name: CensorStatus
nodes.desc: Indicator of whether subject experienced event (0) or was censored (positive integer).

nodes.name: TimeToEventOrigin
nodes.desc: Time-zero for survival analysis - when subject first became at risk.

nodes.name: AnalysisWindow
nodes.desc: Time window for assigning observations to analysis visits.

nodes.name: TreatmentPhaseIndicator
nodes.desc: Flag indicating treatment phase classification (treatment-emergent, on-treatment, pre-treatment, follow-up).

nodes.name: TreatmentPeriodIndicator
nodes.desc: Flag indicating treatment period classification for multi-period studies.

nodes.name: AnalysisSelectionFlag
nodes.desc: Flag indicating whether a record is included in a specific analysis.

nodes.name: OriginalResult
nodes.desc: Verbatim result as originally collected before standardization.

nodes.name: CollectionMethod
nodes.desc: The method, technique, or instrument used to obtain the observation.

nodes.name: BiologicalSpecimen
nodes.desc: The biological sample from which a finding was obtained.

nodes.name: Evaluator
nodes.desc: The person or role who assessed or evaluated the observation.

nodes.name: CompletionStatus
nodes.desc: Whether the observation was collected (DONE/NOT DONE) and reason if not.

nodes.name: AnatomicalSite
nodes.desc: The body location where the observation was made.

nodes.name: DictionaryCoding
nodes.desc: Standardized coding from medical dictionaries (MedDRA, WHODrug).

nodes.name: TherapeuticIntent
nodes.desc: What therapy was planned or intended for the subject according to the protocol.

nodes.name: TherapeuticExposure
nodes.desc: What therapy the subject actually received, accounting for protocol deviations.

nodes.name: TherapyEpoch
nodes.desc: Treatment assignment within a specific study period for multi-period designs.

nodes.name: TherapyGrouping
nodes.desc: Pooled or grouped treatment classification for combined analysis.

nodes.name: CrossoverOrder
nodes.desc: Sequence of treatments in crossover designs.

nodes.name: DosingContext
nodes.desc: Dose magnitude and cumulative exposure information at the record level.

nodes.name: TemporalMilestone
nodes.desc: Key timepoints in the analysis such as analysis date, start date, or end date.

nodes.name: PreciseMoment
nodes.desc: Exact datetime for time-sensitive analyses requiring more precision than date alone.

nodes.name: DataCompleteness
nodes.desc: Indicator of missing or imputed temporal information for data quality assessment.

nodes.name: ElapsedInterval
nodes.desc: Duration or time interval between temporal anchors.

nodes.name: StudyEpoch
nodes.desc: Classification into protocol-defined time segments or periods.

nodes.name: ReferenceSeverity
nodes.desc: Toxicity or severity grade at the reference timepoint (baseline).

nodes.name: ReferenceNormality
nodes.desc: Normal range classification at the reference timepoint (baseline).

nodes.name: CustomThreshold
nodes.desc: Analysis-specific boundary values for custom range classifications.

nodes.name: RiskOnsetMoment
nodes.desc: Precise moment when subject became at risk for the time-to-event analysis.

nodes.name: OriginCertainty
nodes.desc: Confidence in the risk onset timing - indicates whether origin date/time was imputed.

nodes.name: CensoringRationale
nodes.desc: Explanation for why the observation was censored rather than experiencing the event.

nodes.name: EventDescription
nodes.desc: Description of the event of interest or the circumstance that warranted censoring.

nodes.name: EfficacyEligibility
nodes.desc: Subject qualifies for efficacy analyses (Full Analysis Set).

nodes.name: SafetyEligibility
nodes.desc: Subject qualifies for safety analyses (Safety Population).

nodes.name: TreatmentIntention
nodes.desc: Subject assigned with intention to treat (ITT Population).

nodes.name: ProtocolAdherence
nodes.desc: Subject completed per protocol requirements (Per-Protocol Population).

nodes.name: StudyCompletion
nodes.desc: Subject completed the study (Completers Population).

nodes.name: RandomizationStatus
nodes.desc: Subject was randomized into the study.

nodes.name: EnrollmentStatus
nodes.desc: Subject was enrolled into the study.

nodes.name: EventPrimacy
nodes.desc: Whether this is the first occurrence of an event type (for subject, PT, SOC, etc.).
nodes.dcVariable: AOCCFL, AOCCPFL, AOCCSFL, AOCCIFL, AOCCPIFL, AOCCSIFL

nodes.name: SeverityAggregation
nodes.desc: Grouped severity classification for summary analyses.

nodes.name: CausalityAggregation
nodes.desc: Grouped causality classification for relationship analyses.

nodes.name: ToxicityAggregation
nodes.desc: Grouped toxicity grade classification for grading analyses.

nodes.name: MedicalQuery
nodes.desc: Standardized or custom query classification (SMQ or Custom Query).

nodes.name: ObservableProperty
nodes.desc: The characteristic being observed or measured - what is being tested independent of how it's collected or analyzed.

nodes._comment: === PHASE 4I: CO RECORD STRUCTURE LEAF CONCEPTS ===

nodes.name: DataDomain
nodes.desc: Classification of the observation type by SDTM domain.

nodes.name: RecordIdentity
nodes.desc: Unique identification of a record within a data collection.

nodes.name: CrossReference
nodes.desc: Linkage to related observations across domains.

nodes.name: ObservationTaxonomy
nodes.desc: Hierarchical classification of the observation by category and subcategory.

nodes.name: CollectedUnit
nodes.desc: Unit of measure as originally recorded before standardization.

links._comment: === SHARED SEMANTIC CONCEPT RELATIONSHIPS (CO layer to CONCEPT layer) ===

links.d: Baseline indicator is a flag type
Derivation indicator is a flag type
Treatment phase indicator is a flag type
Treatment period indicator is a flag type
Analysis selection flag is a flag type

links._comment: === SHARED SEMANTIC CONCEPT RELATIONSHIPS (DC layer to CONCEPT layer) ===

links.d: DC measure has unit (embedded in PARAM)
DC flags include treatment phase indicator
DC flags include treatment period indicator
DC flags include analysis selection flags
TTE has precise risk onset datetime
TTE has origin data quality indicator

links._comment: === PHASE 4H: OBSERVABLE PROPERTY RELATIONSHIPS (shared bridging FINDING ↔ DERIVED_MEASURE) ===
=== PHASE 4I: CO RECORD STRUCTURE LEAF RELATIONSHIPS ===
